sn23.1:1.0.1: At Sāvatthī. Then the Venerable Radha approached the Blessed One,sn.iii.189paid homage to him, sat down to one side, and said to him: "Venerable sir, it is said, ‘Māra, Māra.’ In what way, venerable sir, might Māra be?"
sn23.1:1.0.2: "When there is form, Radha, there might be Māra, or the killer, or the one who is killed.Therefore, Radha, see form as Māra, see it as the killer, see it as the one who is killed. See it as a disease, as a tumour, as a dart, as misery, as real misery. Those who see it thus see rightly.
sn23.1:1.0.3: "What, venerable sir, is the purpose of seeing rightly?"
